name: WorldCom, Inc.
description: WorldCom, Inc. was a prominent American telecommunications company that grew to become the second-largest long-distance carrier in the United States. Headquartered in Clinton, Mississippi, and later Ashburn, Virginia, the company was a major player in the internet and telecommunications boom of the 1990s. However, it became infamous for a massive accounting scandal involving billions of dollars in overstated earnings, leading to its bankruptcy filing in 2002. Following its emergence from bankruptcy in 2004, the company rebranded as MCI before being acquired by Verizon Communications in 2006, effectively ending its existence as an independent entity.
